why_it_fails: Looks like any city in America. Destroys local credibility and trust with Ocala audience who recognize fake imagery immediately.
correct_approach: Use only Ocala/Marion County specific imagery: Spanish moss on oaks, horse farms with white fences, Silver Springs, downtown Ocala Square, flat Florida terrain with golden light.
applies_to: All pages, covers, hero sections, publication pages

why_it_fails: Gold, forest green, navy, or generic real estate palettes signal franchise or corporate — opposite of locally rooted independent media company.
correct_approach: Stick to exact palette: #090C0B dark, #00B7CE teal, #FAF8F4 cream, #FFFFFF white, #444444 body, #CCCCCC dividers. No exceptions.

why_it_fails: Overwhelms readers. Local media audiences scan quickly — if a page feels heavy they bounce. Competing elements fight for attention and nothing wins.
correct_approach: Every section needs breathing room. Cream backgrounds between content blocks. Max 3 visual elements per section. Let teal accents guide the eye to one CTA per view.
applies_to: Website sections, PDF pages, social graphics

why_it_fails: Content that says Florida instead of Marion County or Ocala feels generic. Audience knows immediately you are not from here.
correct_approach: Always reference Ocala, Marion County, or specific local landmarks. Use neighborhood names, local business references, and county-specific data.

why_it_fails: Formal punctuation that feels academic and stiff. GRM voice is warm and conversational — editorial but approachable, not newspaper-formal.
correct_approach: Use commas, periods, or line breaks instead. Short punchy sentences over long compound structures. Read copy aloud — if it sounds like a textbook, rewrite.
applies_to: All body copy, captions, pull quotes

why_it_fails: Two headlines at the same level sitting side by side or stacked without content between them. Creates a visual wall that readers skip past entirely.
correct_approach: Always separate headlines with body content, imagery, or whitespace. Alternate content types: headline then body then image then headline.
applies_to: PDF layouts, website sections, card grids

why_it_fails: Badge overlapping or crowding a headline makes both unreadable. Looks rushed and unprofessional — kills credibility for a media company.
correct_approach: Minimum 8pt clear space between badge bottom and headline top. Badge draws above headline with gap of 22pt or more. Test at 150% zoom.
applies_to: PDF covers, section openers, card headers

why_it_fails: Leading with a publication name without GRM above it confuses the brand architecture. Audience does not know who the publisher is.
correct_approach: GRM is always the parent publisher on every page. Publication name is secondary. Content title is tertiary. Never let a content title outrank the GRM brand.
applies_to: Covers, footers, website navigation, about pages

why_it_fails: Photos that do not maintain aspect ratio look amateurish. Signals the creator does not care about quality — fatal for a media company brand.
correct_approach: Always use fill-and-crop. Scale to fill frame and clip overflow. Never stretch to fit. Minimum 800px wide for full-bleed and 600px for strips.

why_it_fails: Reusing the same photo in FOUNDER and LISTINGS or across pages within one document looks lazy and breaks the premium editorial feel.
correct_approach: Every photo must be unique within a document and across paired documents. Cover hero, publication pages, and pull quote backgrounds all require distinct images.

pattern: Mountains or non-Florida terrain in photos
why_it_fails: Instant credibility killer. Ocala is flat horse country. Any hill or mountain in the background tells locals this content was not made for them.
correct_approach: Flat terrain only. Verify every photo: no hills, no rocky landscapes, no Pacific Northwest trees, no Northeast architecture. Must pass the Marion County test.

pattern: Dark overlay on photos that do not need it
why_it_fails: Unnecessary dark overlay dims beautiful Florida photography and makes the page feel heavy. Only needed when white text must be readable over a photo.
correct_approach: Only add dark overlay at 0.55-0.72 alpha when quote text must be white over a photo. If text is dark on a light background or no text overlays the photo, skip the overlay.
